scam_reports.description: Fake spice merchants charging 10x market rate to tourists and switching products after purchase.
Friendship bracelet scam near Sacré-Cœur - men forcing bracelets on tourists and demanding money.
Grand Palace is closed scam by tuk-tuk drivers to take you to overpriced jewelry shops.
Fake gladiators charging €50 for a photo near the Colosseum and becoming aggressive if refused.
Bird poop scam: someone squirts mustard on your bag, another person 'helps' clean it while picking your pocket.
Found ring scam: person 'finds' a gold ring on the street, offers it to you for cash claiming they need money for food.
CD scammers in Times Square forcing 'free' CDs into your hands then demanding a 'donation' of $20.
Kind stranger inviting you for a drink at a local bar, then leaving you with a $500 bill and bouncers blocking the exit.
Money changer scam: bait-and-switch with small notes and fast counting to shortchange you by 100,000 IDR.
Helping at the Pyramids: 'free' camel ride or photo help that quickly turns into a demand for €100 tips.
Phone snatchers on dirt bikes near Copacabana beach. Keep phones hidden and avoid using them in the open.
Henna artists grabbing hands and starting a tattoo without consent, then demanding payment for the 'service'.
Wreath or flower sellers placing items on your table at outdoor cafes and demanding high prices if you touch them.
Dishonest currency exchange offices showing mid-market rates but charging 28% commission in small print.
Overpriced 'hidden' service fees at restaurants near St. Marks Square. Always check the menu for 'Coperto'.
Unlicensed safari guides promising 'guaranteed' tiger sightings and taking you into unauthorized buffer zones.
Fake 'eco-tours' that operate without permits and disturb wildlife. Always verify the guide's ICMBio license.
Spiked drinks in Roppongi district bars. Be wary of 'free' entry offers from touts on the street.
Taxi 'Express Kidnappings': avoid hailing green cabs on the street. Only use 'Sitio' taxis or app-based rides.
Art prints on the floor: scammers place posters on the ground in crowded squares, then demand money if you step on them.
Charity/Orphanage tour scams: being taken to 'local charities' where you are pressured to buy overpriced supplies.
Counterfeit luxury watch/bag sellers in Deira Gold Souk pulling you into back rooms with 'exclusive' replicas.
Electronics repair scam: shops in Sim Lim Square quoting low prices then adding 'diagnostic fees' of hundreds of dollars.
Three-card Monte players near Alexanderplatz. They use shills to make you think you can win. You cannot.
Fake opera ticket sellers dressed as Mozart outside the Opera House selling tickets for low-quality student concerts.
ATM 'Help': scammers offer to 'help' you bypass fees at ATMs while using card skimmers or memorizing your PIN.
Uncertified boat charters offering 'cheap' trips to Curieuse Island but lacking safety equipment and insurance.
Fake tea ceremony experiences that are just overpriced tea serving without any traditional context or host certification.
Overpriced 'Indigenous' art that is mass-produced and not authentic. Only buy from certified Aboriginal-owned galleries.
Fake police officers asking to see your passport and 'checking' your wallet for counterfeit bills, then stealing cash.
Unofficial firewood sales outside the park which may carry invasive pests. Always use park-approved wood.
Night tour touts selling 'private' tours on public roads where no wildlife is likely to be seen.
Overpriced 'Cuckoo Clock' exports: verify the VdS certificate to ensure it's a genuine mechanical clock from the region.
Free 'makeover' offers in Myeongdong that lead to high-pressure sales of thousands of dollars in skincare products.
Taxi drivers overcharging for short trips from the Main Station. Use official 'Tick Tack' or 'AAA' taxi stands.
The 'Rose' scam: man hands you or your partner a rose 'for free', then demands €5 once you've accepted it.
Self-proclaimed safari booking agents in hotels who are actually middlemen taking a 40% cut for subpar tours.
Corrupt police stops near Uluwatu: officers targeting tourists on scooters for 'fines' without official paperwork.
Fake comedy show ticket sellers claiming big names like John Mulaney are appearing at small, obscure clubs.
Tailor shop 'lucky day' scam: your tuk-tuk driver claims it's a special day for cheap suits. It's a high-commission trap.
